seoTitle: ClickEle Power Transformers & Inductors | Magnetic Components | LiTong Distributor
seoDescription: Explore ClickEle power transformers, high-frequency transformers, power inductors, current transformers, and EMI filters. Authorized distributor with technical support.

faqs.question: What are the main product categories offered by ClickEle?
faqs.answer: ClickEle specializes in magnetic components and power solutions with four main product categories: (1) Power Transformers - high-frequency transformers for switching power supplies, DC-DC converters, and inverter applications with power ratings from 1W to 10kW; (2) Power Inductors - SMD and through-hole inductors for power applications including chokes, filters, and energy storage with inductance ranges from 0.1μH to 100mH; (3) Current Transformers - precision current sensing transformers for power monitoring, protection relays, and energy meters with accuracy classes from 0.1 to 3.0; (4) EMI Filters - common mode chokes, differential mode chokes, and EMI suppression filters for conducted noise attenuation. These products serve industrial, automotive, telecom, and renewable energy markets.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review each product category to find magnetic components matching your power requirements, frequency range, and mounting preferences.

faqs.question: How do I select the right ClickEle power transformer?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right ClickEle power transformer requires considering: (1) Power rating - select transformer with 20-30% margin over maximum load power; (2) Input voltage range - AC mains (85-264VAC) or DC bus voltage; (3) Output voltage and current - multiple outputs if needed; (4) Switching frequency - typical ranges 50kHz-500kHz for high-frequency transformers; (5) Topology - flyback, forward, half-bridge, full-bridge, LLC resonant; (6) Isolation requirements - basic, reinforced, or functional isolation; (7) Temperature rise - ambient temperature and cooling conditions; (8) Safety certifications - UL, VDE, CE for your target markets. ClickEle provides design guides and FAE support to help optimize transformer selection.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use our transformer selection guide or contact FAE with your power supply specifications for personalized recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the difference between power inductors and chokes?
faqs.answer: ClickEle power inductors and chokes serve different functions in power circuits: (1) Power Inductors - used for energy storage in DC-DC converters (buck, boost, buck-boost), smooth output current, key parameters are inductance, saturation current, and DCR; (2) Chokes - used for filtering AC or pulsating DC, block high-frequency noise while passing DC or low-frequency signals, key parameters are impedance at target frequency and current rating; (3) Common Mode Chokes - suppress common mode noise in power lines and data lines; (4) Differential Mode Chokes - suppress differential mode noise. Both use similar magnetic core materials (ferrite, iron powder, amorphous) but are optimized for different applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose power inductors for DC-DC converter energy storage. Choose chokes for filtering and noise suppression.

faqs.question: Which ClickEle current transformer should I choose for my application?
faqs.answer: For current sensing applications, ClickEle offers several current transformer types: (1) PCB mount CTs - compact size for control boards, current ranges 1A-50A; (2) Panel mount CTs - higher current capability 50A-1000A, through-hole mounting; (3) Split-core CTs - retrofit applications without disconnecting conductors; (4) Precision CTs - accuracy class 0.1-0.5 for metering applications; (5) Protection CTs - accuracy class 1-3 for overcurrent protection. Key selection criteria: (1) Primary current range - select CT with rated primary current 1.2-1.5x maximum expected current; (2) Turns ratio - determines secondary current (typically 1A or 5A); (3) Burden resistor - calculate based on desired output voltage; (4) Accuracy class - metering needs higher accuracy than protection.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select based on current range and accuracy requirements. Contact FAE for protection relay or energy meter applications.

faqs.question: What core materials does ClickEle use for transformers and inductors?
faqs.answer: ClickEle uses various magnetic core materials optimized for different applications: (1) Ferrite (Mn-Zn, Ni-Zn) - high-frequency applications up to 1MHz, low core loss, used in switching power supplies; (2) Iron Powder - cost-effective for inductors, higher core loss but good saturation characteristics; (3) Amorphous Metal - excellent for high-frequency transformers, very low core loss; (4) Nanocrystalline - superior performance for EMI filters and high-frequency applications; (5) Silicon Steel (EI, C-core) - line frequency transformers (50/60Hz), high saturation flux density; (6) Sendust/Kool Mu - powder cores for high-current inductors with low AC loss. Core material selection depends on frequency, flux density, temperature, and cost requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ferrite for high-frequency SMPS, silicon steel for line frequency, amorphous for premium efficiency applications.

categories.name: Power Transformers
categories.description: ClickEle power transformers provide efficient power conversion for switching power supplies, DC-DC converters, and inverter applications. The portfolio includes high-frequency transformers with various topologies and power ratings.
categories.longDescription: ClickEle power transformers are designed for high-efficiency power conversion in industrial, automotive, telecom, and renewable energy applications. As an authorized ClickEle distributor, LiTong provides comprehensive transformer selection guidance, magnetic design support, and application engineering services. The product range includes flyback transformers, forward converters, LLC resonant transformers, and push-pull transformers with power ratings from 1W to 10kW. These transformers feature high-quality ferrite cores, optimized winding designs, and are certified to international safety standards.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select ClickEle Power Transformers
selectionGuide.description: Consider power rating, input/output voltages, switching frequency, and topology when selecting ClickEle transformers.

faqs.question: What is the efficiency of ClickEle power transformers?
faqs.answer: ClickEle power transformer efficiency varies by product series and operating conditions: (1) Small signal transformers (1-10W) - 85-92% efficiency; (2) Medium power transformers (10-100W) - 92-96% efficiency; (3) High power transformers (100W-1kW) - 96-98% efficiency; (4) Efficiency depends on load - typically peaks at 50-75% load; (5) Core material affects efficiency - amorphous cores offer 20-30% lower loss than ferrite at high frequency. Losses include core loss (hysteresis and eddy current) and copper loss (winding resistance). For high-efficiency designs, ClickEle offers amorphous and nanocrystalline core options.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select transformer with efficiency rating matching your application requirements. Amorphous cores for premium efficiency.

faqs.question: How do I calculate transformer temperature rise?
faqs.answer: Transformer temperature rise calculation: (1) Total losses = Core loss + Copper loss; (2) Core loss from datasheet at operating flux density and frequency; (3) Copper loss = I² × DCR (primary and secondary); (4) Temperature rise = Total losses × Thermal resistance; (5) Typical thermal resistance 20-40°C/W for small transformers, 5-15°C/W for large transformers with heatsinking. Example: 10W losses with 20°C/W thermal resistance = 200°C rise (unacceptable). Solutions: (1) Increase conductor size to reduce copper loss; (2) Select lower loss core material; (3) Improve thermal design with heatsink or forced air; (4) Use larger core size to reduce flux density.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate temperature rise during design phase. Ensure maximum temperature stays within insulation class limits.

faqs.question: What safety certifications do ClickEle transformers have?
faqs.answer: ClickEle transformers carry major international safety certifications: (1) UL 5085 - US standard for low voltage transformers; (2) IEC 61558 - International safety standard for power transformers; (3) VDE 0570 - German safety standard; (4) TUV - European safety certification; (5) CCC - China Compulsory Certification; (6) CE - European conformity marking; (7) UL 1446 - Insulation systems recognition. Transformers are designed with appropriate creepage and clearance distances for reinforced or basic isolation. Certification files and test reports are available for each product series. Custom transformers can be designed to meet specific safety requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify required certifications for your market. ClickEle can provide certified transformers for most global markets.

faqs.question: What is the difference between flyback and forward transformer?
faqs.answer: ClickEle flyback and forward transformers have different designs: (1) Flyback - energy stored in core during on-time, delivered to output during off-time, air gap required in core, simpler design, good for <150W, higher peak currents; (2) Forward - energy transferred directly during on-time, no air gap, requires reset winding or diode, better for higher power, lower peak currents; (3) Flyback uses single winding for each output, forward needs separate rectifier for each output; (4) Flyback has discontinuous secondary current, forward has continuous current. ClickEle offers both types optimized for their respective topologies with proper gap design for flyback and tight coupling for forward.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use flyback for <150W and cost-sensitive designs. Use forward for higher power and better efficiency.

faqs.question: How do I specify a custom transformer?
faqs.answer: For custom transformer specifications, provide ClickEle with: (1) Input voltage range - nominal, minimum, maximum; (2) Output requirements - voltage, current, regulation, number of outputs; (3) Power rating - continuous and peak power; (4) Switching frequency - operating frequency and topology; (5) Size constraints - maximum dimensions; (6) Temperature requirements - ambient range and max temperature rise; (7) Isolation requirements - working voltage, test voltage, creepage/clearance; (8) Safety standards - required certifications; (9) Quantities - prototype and production volumes. ClickEle engineering team can optimize the design for cost, size, or efficiency based on your priorities. Typical custom design cycle is 2-4 weeks for prototypes.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong FAE with your specifications for custom transformer design support.

products.name: High-Frequency Power Transformer
products.shortDescription: 100W flyback transformer with 120V input, 24V/4A output, 100kHz switching, EF25 core.
descriptionParagraphs: The CL-EF25-120-24-100 is a high-frequency power transformer designed for flyback and forward converter applications up to 100W.
Featuring an EF25 ferrite core with optimized winding design, this transformer provides excellent coupling and low leakage inductance.
The 120V input to 24V/4A output configuration is ideal for industrial power supplies, LED drivers, and DC-DC converter applications.

faeReview.author: Zhang Wei
faeReview.title: Applications Engineer - Power Systems
faeReview.content: The CL-EF25 series has been my go-to recommendation for 50-150W industrial power supplies. The EF25 core offers a good balance of power handling and low profile - perfect for 1U rackmount designs. The 3kV isolation is sufficient for most industrial applications, and the >95% efficiency helps meet energy standards. I've used these in dozens of designs from LED drivers to motor control power supplies. One tip: the leakage inductance is well controlled at <5μH, which minimizes snubber losses in flyback applications. For higher power or better thermal performance, consider the CL-PQ series. The standard pinout makes it easy to second-source if needed.
faeReview.highlight: Reliable 100W transformer with excellent efficiency and low profile design

alternativeParts.reason: ClickEle offers higher power rating at competitive price

Featuring a PQ32 ferrite core with sectional bobbin design, this transformer provides excellent isolation and low interwinding capacitance.
The 400V input to 48V/10A output is ideal for server power supplies, telecom rectifiers, and EV charger applications.

I suggest considering thermal management and proper PCB layout for optimal results.

categories.name: Power Inductors
categories.description: ClickEle power inductors provide energy storage and filtering for DC-DC converters, power supplies, and EMI filtering applications. Available in SMD and through-hole packages with various core materials.
categories.longDescription: ClickEle power inductors are designed for energy storage, filtering, and EMI suppression in switching power supplies and DC-DC converters. The product range includes shielded and unshielded SMD inductors, toroidal inductors, and common mode chokes with inductance values from 0.1μH to 100mH and current ratings up to 100A. These inductors feature high-quality ferrite, iron powder, and amorphous cores optimized for low core loss and high saturation current. Available through BeiLuo authorized distributor with comprehensive selection guide.

products.name: SMD Power Inductor
products.shortDescription: 4.7μH shielded SMD inductor, 10x10mm, 10A saturation current, 3.5mΩ DCR. Ideal for industrial and consumer applicatio...
descriptionParagraphs: The CL-PI-100-4R7 is a shielded SMD power inductor designed for high-current DC-DC converter applications.
Featuring a ferrite drum core with magnetic shielding, this inductor provides excellent EMI performance and low magnetic coupling.
The 4.7μH inductance with 10A saturation current is ideal for output filters in buck converters up to 20A output.

faeReview.author: Wang Hua
faeReview.title: Power Electronics Engineer
faeReview.content: The CL-PI-100-4R7 is my standard recommendation for 10-20A buck converters. The 4.7μH hits the sweet spot for 300-500kHz switching frequency - good ripple current without excessive DCR loss. The 3.5mΩ DCR means only 0.35W loss at 10A, keeping efficiency high. The shielding is effective - I've never had EMI issues with this series. AEC-Q200 qualification makes it suitable for automotive applications. One tip: the thermal rating is conservative - you can run higher current with good PCB copper area for heat sinking. For higher current, the CL-PI-130 series offers 15A+ capability. Overall excellent value for the performance.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent 10A inductor with low DCR and effective shielding for automotive apps

categories.name: EMI Filters
categories.description: ClickEle EMI filters provide effective suppression of conducted electromagnetic interference in power lines and data lines. The portfolio includes common mode chokes, differential mode chokes, and integrated EMI filter modules.
categories.longDescription: ClickEle EMI filters are designed to suppress conducted electromagnetic interference in switching power supplies, motor drives, and electronic equipment. The product range includes common mode chokes for suppressing common mode noise, differential mode chokes for differential noise, and integrated filter modules with capacitors and chokes. These filters help equipment meet CISPR, FCC, and EN EMC standards. Available through BeiLuo authorized distributor with comprehensive selection guide.
